a:T59cc,<div class="question">
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;">What is the oxidation state of the metal ion and charge of the complex ion in [Co(NH<sub>3</sub>)<sub>4</sub>Cl<sub>2</sub>]Cl?</span></p>
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;"><img height="239" src="data:image/png;base64," width="412"/></span></p>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/chemistry/topic/13/","children":["← back to ","Chemistry"," topic ","13"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"19M.1.HL.TZ2.8"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Chemistry"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","1"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"19M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","13-1-first-row-d-block-elements"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>C</p>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>This was one of the most challenging questions on the paper and it discriminated well between high scoring and low scoring candidates. 57 % of the candidates were able to use the formula of the compound to deduce the oxidation state of the metal ion and the charge of the complex ion. The most commonly chosen distractor was B where the charge of the complex ion was correct but the charge of the metal ion was not. Some teachers commented that the question was challenging but reasonable.</p>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
